draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer minimal color palettes that reduce cognitive load and emphasize content
facade.label: Monochrome workspace
facade.acceptImplies: Move toward desaturated, neutral-dominant interfaces with selective color accents
facade.rejectImplies: Explore richer color systems, vibrant gradients, or multi-hue visual hierarchies

evidence.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer minimal color palettes that reduce cognitive load and emphasize content
evidence.implication: Move toward desaturated, neutral-dominant interfaces with selective color accents

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer friendly, accessible language over corporate formality in interface copy and messaging
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, approachable copy; use 'we' and direct address; avoid jargon and stiff enterprise language
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward polished, authoritative voice; use passive constructions; emphasize expertise and credentials over relatability

facade.hypothesis: Users building an 'ai workspace' may oscillate between wanting calming, organic softness versus crisp, efficient digital precision. Testing warm, rounded aesthetics reveals if they lean toward approachability or functionality.
facade.label: Soft corners and shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Leaning toward human-centered, approachable atmosphere—possibly softer UI, gradient transitions, gentler interactions
facade.rejectImplies: Preference for clarity and efficiency—possibly sharper interfaces, high contrast, minimal ornamentation

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer metric-focused dashboards over narrative summaries
facade.label: Numbers in cards
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should prioritize quantified outputs (token count, latency, cost per query) in scannable card format
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should favor contextual prose, explanations, and story-driven insights over bare numbers

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, momentum-based scroll interactions over instant viewport jumps in an AI workspace where continuous context matters
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid transitions vs snappy instant responses
facade.label: Smooth scrolling
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize eased, physics-based motion; users value feeling of continuity when navigating between tasks and conversation history
facade.rejectImplies: Users want fast, direct navigation with minimal animation overhead; scrolling should be utility-first, not experiential

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/supportive tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics
Whether user likes smooth/gradual transitions vs instant/snappy response to their actions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/supportive tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/gradual transitions vs instant/snappy response to their actions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: An AI workspace benefits from persistent left navigation paired with modular card-based content areas, reducing cognitive load by anchoring context while keeping work units scannable.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation anchoring vs. flat hierarchy; modularity (cards) vs. continuous surfaces (lists/streams)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values spatial consistency and quick context-switching. Lean into persistent left sidebar, card grids for projects/threads, minimal scrolling per view.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers either flatter navigation (tabs/breadcrumbs) or flowing continuous content (lists/streams). Explore top-tab layout or scrollable single-pane alternatives next.

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace interfaces benefit from rounded, gentle geometry that reduces visual harshness and creates a more approachable, less intimidating aesthetic for complex tool interactions
facade.label: Soft corners
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize border-radius and smooth curves throughout UI components; avoid hard corners and angular shapes
facade.rejectImplies: Sharp, angular geometry is acceptable; users may prefer clean lines and defined boundaries for clarity in dense workspaces

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, prompts, and outputs with a clean, productivity-focused design.
